edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:3cbb5a8d652d
edges.original_sentence: Results indicate that invasion of C. arvense negatively affects fir emergence and early survival, and may further contribute to continued balsam fir regeneration failure independent of future moose densities.

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:96767da46c1e
edges.original_sentence: Its management is complicated by the lack of viable control techniques and an overarching issue of gap regeneration failure attributed to browsing by non-native moose (Alces alces).

source_spans.text: Its management is complicated by the lack of viable control techniques and an overarching issue of gap regeneration failure attributed to browsing by non-native moose (Alces alces).

source_document.title: Impacts of non-native plant and animal invaders on gap regeneration in a protected boreal forest

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:c124f84dad72
edges.original_sentence: However, older fir seedlings transplanted into thistle monocultures experienced a positive facilitative effect due to the protection thistle provided against small mammal herbivory.

source_spans.text: However, older fir seedlings transplanted into thistle monocultures experienced a positive facilitative effect due to the protection thistle provided against small mammal herbivory.

source_document.title: Impacts of non-native plant and animal invaders on gap regeneration in a protected boreal forest

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:0edfeb082d01
edges.original_sentence: Restoration actions that combine moose density reductions with the planting of fir seedlings offers the most viable long-term strategy to re-establish the native forest canopy in thistle-invaded gaps and would likely lead to the eventual decline of shade-intolerant C. arvense.

source_spans.text: Restoration actions that combine moose density reductions with the planting of fir seedlings offers the most viable long-term strategy to re-establish the native forest canopy in thistle-invaded gaps and would likely lead to the eventual decline of shade-intolerant C. arvense.

source_document.title: Impacts of non-native plant and animal invaders on gap regeneration in a protected boreal forest

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:104bca820a56
edges.original_sentence: C. arvense is shade intolerant: growth of its seedlings is considerably reduced in 60–70% of full daylight levels
